title: Phase 0: Refactor infra/ into provider modules + switch-dispatch Deploy()
description:
## What to build

Refactor `infra/` from its current AWS-only flat layout into the provider-module shape that the multi-cloud spec depends on. **No new cloud resources** — the gate is a zero-diff `pulumi preview` against existing AWS stacks.

A 10-task implementation plan already exists at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-phase0-foundation.md`.

In scope:
- Create `pkg/types/` with shared output structs (`NetworkOutputs`, `DatabaseOutputs`, `CacheOutputs`, `StorageOutputs`, `IAMOutputs`, `StreamingOutputs`, `ComputeOutputs`, `SecretsOutputs`, `EdgeOutputs`).
- Move existing `pkg/{database,cache,network,storage,compute,secrets,loadbalancer,observability,streaming,cicd,waf,dns}` modules into `pkg/aws/` and update them to return the shared output structs.
- Rewrite `Deploy()` in `main.go` to dispatch via `switch cfg.CloudProvider` and compose stages via shared types.
- Update all test imports.

Out of scope: any GCP code, any behavioral change (resource shapes/names/properties must be byte-for-byte identical to today).

## HITL

Requires a human review checkpoint at merge — the zero-diff gate is a manual eyeball of `pulumi preview` output across `dev`, `staging`, and `prod`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/types/` exports all output structs the spec lists, with documented field meaning.
- [ ] All current `pkg/*` modules live under `pkg/aws/` and return shared output structs.
- [ ] `main.go` `Deploy()` uses `switch cfg.CloudProvider` and composes via shared types.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack dev` shows zero diff vs. main.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack staging` shows zero diff vs. main.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack prod` shows zero diff vs. main.
- [ ] `just test-infra` passes.
- [ ] All existing test imports updated; no test removed.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 0)
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-phase0-foundation.md`

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Phase 2: Replace Confluent Schema Registry with Redpanda in Docker Compose
description:
## What to build

Replace `confluentinc/cp-schema-registry` in the local `docker-compose.yml` with Redpanda's built-in Schema Registry, then verify all existing Rust and Go Kafka producer/consumer tests pass against it with no application-code edits. This is the wire-compatibility gate that must clear before any Redpanda Cloud deployment.

In scope:
- Replace the Schema Registry container with Redpanda in the compose file used by streaming integration tests.
- Rerun `cargo test` Kafka-touching tests and Go pipeline Kafka tests against the new stack.
- Document any subtle Schema Registry HTTP API differences the test suite exercises.

Out of scope: any cloud Redpanda resources, any Pulumi changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docker-compose up` brings up Redpanda with built-in Schema Registry on the same port the Confluent image occupied.
- [ ] All Rust Kafka-touching tests in `experimentation-ingest` and `experimentation-pipeline` pass with no app-code edits.
- [ ] All Go pipeline Kafka tests pass with no app-code edits.
- [ ] Any compatibility caveats are documented in a streaming-integration guide.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 2 + Testing Strategy → Streaming integration test)

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Phase 1: GCP network module (VPC, firewall, Service Directory, VPC connector)
description:
## What to build

GCP provider-module for networking. Returns `types.NetworkOutputs` so it composes into `Deploy()` without touching the AWS path.

In scope:
- VPC with public/private subnets across the configured region's zones.
- Firewall rules covering the security-group equivalents the AWS module exposes (keys: `ecs`, `rds`, `redis`, etc.).
- Service Directory namespace for service discovery (Cloud Map analogue).
- Serverless VPC Access connector for Cloud Run → VPC traffic.
- Pulumi unit tests + parameterized topology test (AWS + GCP) asserting `NetworkOutputs` field shapes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/network.go` returns `types.NetworkOutputs` with all fields populated.
- [ ] Topology test runs Deploy with mocks for both `cloudProvider: aws` and `cloudProvider: gcp` and passes.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the network slice.
- [ ] Firewall rules expose the same security-group keys as the AWS module.
- [ ] VPC connector reachable from Cloud Run service template (verified by #8 once it lands).

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 1 + AWS→GCP service mapping)

## Blocked by

- #477 (Phase 0 refactor)

title: Phase 1: GCP storage module (Cloud Storage buckets)
description:
## What to build

GCP provider-module for object storage. Returns `types.StorageOutputs`. Provisions the same logical buckets the AWS S3 module exposes (`data`, `mlflow`, `logs`).

In scope:
- Cloud Storage buckets matching the AWS bucket inventory.
- Lifecycle rules + versioning parity with the AWS module.
- IAM bindings via Workload Identity (consumed by compute services later).
- `BucketRef` populated as `gs://...` URIs (vs. ARNs on AWS).
- Topology test asserts `StorageOutputs` is populated for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/storage.go` returns `types.StorageOutputs` with `DataBucketName/Ref`, `MlflowBucketName/Ref`, `LogsBucketName/Ref`.
- [ ] Lifecycle and versioning rules match AWS bucket equivalents.
- [ ] Topology test runs both providers and asserts bucket count + names.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the storage slice.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 1 + Shared Output Types)

## Blocked by

- #477 (Phase 0 refactor)

title: Phase 1: GCP secrets module (Secret Manager)
description:
## What to build

GCP provider-module for secret material. Returns `types.SecretsOutputs`. Provisions Secret Manager secrets for database creds, Kafka/Redpanda creds, Redis auth, and service auth — the same set the AWS Secrets Manager module exposes.

In scope:
- Secret Manager secrets for: `database`, `kafka`, `redis`, `auth`.
- IAM bindings allowing the compute service accounts to read each secret.
- `SecretRef` fields populated as `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` paths (vs. ARNs on AWS).
- Topology test asserts every compute-input secret has a creator in the same test run (per spec gap mitigation #3).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/secrets.go` returns `types.SecretsOutputs` with all four ref fields populated.
- [ ] Topology test verifies every secret referenced in compute module inputs was created by the secrets module in the same run, for both providers.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the secrets slice.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 1 + Testing Strategy → Secret injection failure modes)

## Blocked by

- #477 (Phase 0 refactor)

title: Phase 1: GCP CI/CD — Artifact Registry + dual-push image pipeline
description:
## What to build

Stand up Artifact Registry (the GCP analogue of ECR) and extend the CI image pipeline to push the same multi-arch tag/digest to both ECR and Artifact Registry in parallel. Required so any Cloud Run deploy has an image to pull.

In scope:
- Pulumi `pkg/gcp/cicd.go` provisioning Artifact Registry repos for all 9 Kaizen services + M4b.
- CI workflow change: build once (already multi-arch), push to ECR and Artifact Registry in parallel with identical tags.
- IAM binding: GCP service accounts can pull from Artifact Registry; AWS roles continue to pull from ECR.
- Documented credential rotation playbook for the Artifact Registry push role.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/cicd.go` creates an Artifact Registry repo for every service that has an ECR repo today.
- [ ] CI builds + pushes to both registries on every merge to `main`; both tags resolve to the same digest.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the cicd slice.
- [ ] Manual test: a Cloud Run service can `docker pull` from Artifact Registry using the provisioned service account.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Container Image Strategy)

## Blocked by

- #477 (Phase 0 refactor)

title: Phase 2: Redpanda streaming module via Pulumi-Terraform bridge
description:
## What to build

Cloud-agnostic Redpanda streaming module under `pkg/streaming/`. Returns `types.StreamingOutputs`. Dispatched on `cfg.StreamingProvider` (so an AWS tenant can opt into Redpanda later without changing cloud provider). Uses the Redpanda Terraform provider via Pulumi's TF bridge.

In scope:
- `pkg/streaming/redpanda.go` provisioning a Redpanda Cloud cluster, users/ACLs, and topics matching the existing MSK topic inventory.
- Schema Registry endpoint surfaced via `StreamingOutputs.SchemaRegistryUrl` (Redpanda's built-in registry).
- `Deploy()` dispatch on `streamingProvider` (`msk` → AWS module, `redpanda` → this module).
- Topology test parameterized over `streamingProvider`.

Out of scope: self-hosted Redpanda on VMs (fallback path; document but do not implement).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/streaming/redpanda.go` provisions cluster + users/ACLs + topics via the Pulumi-TF bridge.
- [ ] `Deploy()` dispatches correctly on `streamingProvider` for both `msk` and `redpanda`.
- [ ] Topology test asserts `StreamingOutputs` is populated identically by either provider.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds with `streamingProvider: redpanda`.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Streaming + Redpanda Deployment Model)

## Blocked by

- #477 (Phase 0 refactor)
- #478 (Schema Registry compat — must prove wire compatibility before any cloud Redpanda deployment)

title: Phase 1: GCP database module (Cloud SQL PostgreSQL)
description:
## What to build

GCP provider-module for the relational database. Returns `types.DatabaseOutputs` (`Endpoint`, `InstanceId`). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with regional HA, parity to the AWS RDS module.

In scope:
- `pkg/gcp/database.go` provisioning Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with version + sizing matching RDS configuration.
- Private-IP only (reachable only via VPC connector).
- Backup/PITR config matching RDS.
- IAM binding: compute service accounts can authenticate via IAM-DB auth where applicable.
- Topology test parameterized for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/database.go` returns `types.DatabaseOutputs` with `Endpoint` (host:port) and `InstanceId`.
- [ ] Topology test asserts DB is reachable only from the private subnet path.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the database slice.
- [ ] Backup retention + PITR window matches the AWS RDS configuration.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 1 + Shared Output Types → DatabaseOutputs)

## Blocked by

- #479 (GCP network)
- #481 (GCP secrets)

title: Phase 1: GCP cache module (Memorystore Redis)
description:
## What to build

GCP provider-module for the in-memory cache. Returns `types.CacheOutputs` (`Endpoint`). Memorystore Redis with HA + auth, parity to the AWS ElastiCache module.

In scope:
- `pkg/gcp/cache.go` provisioning Memorystore Redis with the same node count + version as ElastiCache.
- Private-IP only via VPC connector.
- AUTH enabled; password sourced from `SecretsOutputs.RedisSecretRef`.
- Endpoint surfaced as `redis://host:port`.
- Topology test parameterized for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/cache.go` returns `types.CacheOutputs` with `Endpoint` populated.
- [ ] Topology test asserts cache is reachable only via VPC connector.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the cache slice.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 1)

## Blocked by

- #479 (GCP network)
- #481 (GCP secrets)

title: Phase 1: GCP Cloud Run service template + Workload Identity + Service Directory registration helper
description:
## What to build

Reusable Cloud Run service factory under `pkg/gcp/compute.go`. Lets per-service deploys (M1, M2, M3, ...) call a single helper to provision a Cloud Run service with consistent VPC connector binding, Workload Identity binding, secret injection, and Service Directory registration. This is the compute analogue of the AWS ECS task-definition factory.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(ctx, cfg, name, opts)` helper returning a Cloud Run URL into `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints`.
- VPC connector wired automatically (consumed from `NetworkOutputs`).
- Workload Identity service account creation per service, plus IAM bindings to required secrets/buckets/SQL/Redis (driven by `opts`).
- Service Directory endpoint registration so other services can resolve via the namespace from `NetworkOutputs.ServiceDiscoveryId`.
- Default `min-instances` = 0, with override support (M1/M7 will set 1).
- Topology test verifying every Cloud Run service has the expected Workload Identity binding (per spec gap mitigation #2).

Out of scope: deploying any actual service — that's #10–#17.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Helper exists, takes the spec-required options (image, min/max instances, secret refs, env vars, IAM scopes).
- [ ] Topology test asserts: for each Cloud Run service in the test stack, a Workload Identity binding exists with the expected scopes.
- [ ] Service Directory endpoint registration verified by topology test.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds with at least one trivial test service using the helper.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model → Stateless Services + Testing Strategy → IAM/service account binding drift)

## Blocked by

- #479 (GCP network — VPC connector + Service Directory)
- #482 (GCP CI/CD — Artifact Registry must exist for service to have an image source)

title: Phase 1: GCP M4b compute (GCE + persistent disk + autohealing MIG)
description:
## What to build

Stateful M4b deployment on GCE — the GCP analogue of the AWS EC2-in-ASG-of-1 pattern. Must hold M4b's invariants on GCP: single instance, RocksDB snapshot on persistent disk, LMAX single-threaded core, crash recovery < 10s, Kafka consumer for reward events.

In scope:
- `pkg/gcp/compute.go::NewM4bInstance(...)` (or sibling file) returning `types.ComputeOutputs.M4bInstanceId` + `M4bEndpoint`.
- n2-standard-4 instance backed by a 50GB pd-ssd persistent disk.
- Managed Instance Group of size 1 with autohealing.
- Persistent disk reattached on instance recreation (so RocksDB snapshot survives).
- Service Directory endpoint registration for service discovery.
- Topology test parameterized for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/compute.go` (or `m4b.go`) provisions the instance + disk + MIG.
- [ ] Persistent disk survives instance recreation (verified manually or by topology config inspection).
- [ ] `ComputeOutputs.M4bEndpoint` resolves via Service Directory.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the M4b slice.
- [ ] Recovery time validated by #24 chaos test (separate issue).

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model → M4b Policy Service)

## Blocked by

- #479 (GCP network)
- #481 (GCP secrets)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M1 Assignment to Cloud Run (min-instances=1, p99 < 5ms SLA)
description:
## What to build

Wire M1 Assignment into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. M1 holds the strictest latency budget on the platform (p99 < 5ms), so this slice must set `min-instances: 1` to eliminate cold starts.

In scope:
- Stack code calling `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M1 with image from Artifact Registry, `min-instances: 1`, env vars wired from `NetworkOutputs`, `DatabaseOutputs`, `CacheOutputs`, `SecretsOutputs`, and Redpanda bootstrap brokers.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read Redis auth, read Kafka creds.
- Health check verified end-to-end (`/health` green via VPC connector path).

Out of scope: SLA validation under load — that's #23.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M1 Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m1"]`.
- [ ] `min-instances: 1` confirmed via topology test.
- [ ] M1 health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M1 can read DB creds, Redis auth, and Kafka creds from Secret Manager.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model → Cloud Run constraints handled)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #484 (database)
- #485 (cache)
- #481 (secrets)
- #483 (Redpanda module)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M2 Pipeline to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M2 Pipeline (Rust ingest service) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. M2 is a high-throughput Kafka producer; it must reach Redpanda via the VPC connector path.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M2 with default min-instances and elevated max-instances for throughput.
- Env vars: Redpanda bootstrap brokers, Schema Registry URL, Kafka credentials from Secret Manager.
- IAM scopes: read Kafka creds.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

Out of scope: throughput SLA validation — that's #25.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M2 Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m2-pipeline"]`.
- [ ] M2 health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M2 successfully publishes a synthetic event to Redpanda end-to-end.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #481 (secrets)
- #483 (Redpanda module)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M2-Orch to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M2 Orchestration (Go service) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. Stateless coordinator — needs DB for orchestration state and Kafka for event flow.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M2-Orch with default min-instances.
- Env vars: DB endpoint, Redpanda bootstrap brokers, secrets refs.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read Kafka creds.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M2-Orch Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m2-orch"]`.
- [ ] M2-Orch health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M2-Orch can connect to Cloud SQL via the VPC connector.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #484 (database)
- #481 (secrets)
- #483 (Redpanda module)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M3 Metrics to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M3 Metrics (Go service) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. Submits Spark SQL jobs and writes to Delta Lake on Cloud Storage; reads metric definitions from Cloud SQL.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M3 with default min-instances.
- Env vars: DB endpoint, data bucket name + ref (`gs://...`), secrets refs.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read/write data bucket.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M3 Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m3"]`.
- [ ] M3 health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M3 can list and write objects in the data bucket.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #480 (storage)
- #484 (database)
- #481 (secrets)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M4a Analysis to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M4a Analysis (Rust service) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. CPU-intensive batch — needs storage for inputs/outputs and DB for experiment metadata.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M4a with elevated CPU + memory limits.
- Env vars: DB endpoint, data bucket name + ref, secrets refs.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read/write data bucket.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M4a Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m4a"]`.
- [ ] M4a health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M4a can list and write objects in the data bucket.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #480 (storage)
- #484 (database)
- #481 (secrets)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M5 Management to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M5 Management (Rust service per ADR-025) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. CRUD-heavy with cache for hot reads and Kafka for lifecycle events.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M5 with default min-instances.
- Env vars: DB endpoint, Redis endpoint, Redpanda bootstrap brokers, secrets refs.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read Redis auth, read Kafka creds.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

Note: the Go variant of M5 is "kept until deprecation" per CLAUDE.md but the canonical deployment is the Rust crate `experimentation-management` (ADR-025). The Go variant is not deployed to GCP unless the user later requests it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M5 Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m5"]`.
- [ ] M5 health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M5 can connect to Cloud SQL, Memorystore, and Redpanda end-to-end.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #484 (database)
- #485 (cache)
- #481 (secrets)
- #483 (Redpanda module)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M6 UI to Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Wire M6 UI (Next.js 14 SSR) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. Calls the other services through the internal LB / Service Directory; needs only auth secrets for the SSR session layer.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M6 with default min-instances.
- Env vars: backend service URLs (resolved via `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints`), auth secret ref.
- IAM scopes: read auth secret.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] M6 Cloud Run service appears in `ComputeOutputs.ServiceEndpoints["m6"]`.
- [ ] M6 health check returns 200 in a deployed dev stack.
- [ ] M6 can resolve and reach at least one backend service through the Service Directory.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model)

## Blocked by

- #486 (Cloud Run template)
- #481 (secrets)

title: Phase 1: Deploy M7 Flags to Cloud Run (min-instances=1, p99 < 5ms SLA)
description:
## What to build

Wire M7 Flags (Rust service per ADR-024) into the GCP stack via the Cloud Run service template. Same p99 < 5ms profile as M1 — must run with `min-instances: 1`.

In scope:
- `NewCloudRunService(...)` for M7 with `min-instances: 1`.
- Env vars: DB endpoint, Redis endpoint, secrets refs.
- IAM scopes: read DB creds, read Redis auth.
- Health check verified end-to-end.

Out of scope: SLA validation under load — that's #23.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `min-instances: 1` confirmed via topology test.

title: Phase 3: GCP edge module (Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud DNS, managed certs, Cloud Armor)
description:
## What to build

GCP edge layer for the GCP stack. Returns `types.EdgeOutputs`. Provides external HTTPS termination + WAF in front of the Cloud Run services, parity with the AWS ALB + Route53 + ACM + WAF v2 module.

In scope:
- `pkg/gcp/edge.go` provisioning a global external HTTPS Load Balancer with serverless NEG backends pointing at the 8 Cloud Run services.
- Cloud DNS managed zone + records mapping the public hostname to the LB.
- Google-managed SSL certificates auto-provisioned for the hostname.
- Cloud Armor security policy attached, with WAF rules at parity to the AWS WAF v2 ruleset.
- `EdgeOutputs.LoadBalancerDns`, `CertificateRef`, `HostedZoneId` populated.
- Topology test parameterized for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/edge.go` returns `types.EdgeOutputs`.
- [ ] LB routes traffic to all 8 Cloud Run services with the same path prefixes the AWS ALB uses.
- [ ] Cloud Armor rules at WAF parity (manual diff acceptable).
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the edge slice.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 3 + AWS→GCP service mapping)

## Blocked by

- #488 (M1)
- #489 (M2 Pipeline)
- #490 (M2-Orch)
- #491 (M3)
- #492 (M4a)
- #493 (M5)
- #494 (M6)
- #495 (M7)

title: Phase 3: GCP observability (Cloud Logging/Monitoring + Managed Prometheus + Grafana)
description:
## What to build

GCP observability layer for the GCP stack. Cloud Logging + Cloud Monitoring for native cloud telemetry, Managed Service for Prometheus for metrics scraping, and Grafana Cloud for dashboards (same Grafana instance the AWS stack already uses).

In scope:
- `pkg/gcp/observability.go` provisioning Cloud Logging sinks, Cloud Monitoring alert policies parity to CloudWatch alarms, and Managed Prometheus collectors targeting the Cloud Run services.
- Grafana Cloud datasource + dashboards reused from AWS (no fork).
- Log routing for M4b GCE instance + all 8 Cloud Run services.
- Topology test parameterized for both providers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pkg/gcp/observability.go` provisions logging, monitoring alerts, and Managed Prometheus.
- [ ] Existing Grafana dashboards render with GCP data source.
- [ ] Alert policy inventory matches the AWS CloudWatch alarm inventory.
- [ ] `pulumi preview --stack gcp-dev` succeeds for the observability slice.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 3 + AWS→GCP service mapping)

## Blocked by

- #488 (M1)
- #489 (M2 Pipeline)
- #490 (M2-Orch)
- #491 (M3)
- #492 (M4a)
- #493 (M5)
- #494 (M6)
- #495 (M7)

title: Phase 4: Smoke load test — M1/M7 p99 < 5ms on Cloud Run
description:
## What to build

Validate the M1 Assignment and M7 Flags p99 < 5ms SLA on real Cloud Run infrastructure. This is the gap mitigation for cold-start + VPC connector latency risk identified in the spec.

In scope:
- Load test harness deployed against the GCP dev (or first-customer) stack.
- 60-second steady-state run against M1 and M7 with realistic request shape (auth headers, payload sizes from real traffic).
- p99 latency measured at the LB edge and at the service.
- Pass/fail gate: p99 < 5ms at the service level.
- Test wired into a weekly or pre-release CI job.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Load test harness committed (script or k6/wrk config).
- [ ] M1 p99 < 5ms over a 60s steady-state run on Cloud Run.
- [ ] M7 p99 < 5ms over a 60s steady-state run on Cloud Run.
- [ ] CI job runs the test on a schedule and reports pass/fail.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Testing Strategy → Cold start + VPC connector latency)

## Blocked by

- #488 (M1 deploy)
- #495 (M7 deploy)
- #496 (GCP edge)

title: Phase 4: M4b chaos test — kill GCE instance, verify recovery < 10s
description:
## What to build

Validate M4b's < 10s crash-recovery invariant on GCP. Forcefully terminate the GCE instance backing M4b, verify the MIG autohealing recreates it with the persistent disk reattached and RocksDB snapshot intact, and measure end-to-end recovery time.

In scope:
- Chaos script that issues `gcloud compute instances delete --zone=...` (or simulates a kernel panic) against the M4b instance.
- Probes the M4b service endpoint until it returns 200 again.
- Asserts the recovery time < 10s and RocksDB state survived (sample read of pre-crash state).
- Wired into a weekly chaos CI job.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Chaos test committed (script or runner).
- [ ] Recovery time measured < 10s in at least 3 consecutive runs.
- [ ] RocksDB state verified intact after recovery.
- [ ] CI job runs the test on a schedule.

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Compute Model → M4b Policy Service)

## Blocked by

- #487 (M4b GCE compute)

title: Phase 4: M2 throughput test — 100K events/sec via Redpanda on GCP
description:
## What to build

Validate M2 Pipeline can sustain 100K events/sec end-to-end through Redpanda on the GCP stack. This is the headline throughput SLA the platform commits to.

In scope:
- Synthetic event generator publishing at the target rate to M2's gRPC ingest endpoint.
- 5-minute steady-state run.
- Measurement at: ingest acceptance rate, Redpanda producer offset advance, downstream consumer lag.
- Pass/fail gate: 100K events/sec sustained, no message loss, consumer lag bounded.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Throughput harness committed.
- [ ] M2 sustains 100K events/sec for 5 minutes on the GCP dev stack.
- [ ] Zero message loss across the run.
- [ ] Redpanda consumer lag stays bounded (define threshold per Redpanda Cloud sizing).

## References

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-20-multi-cloud-gcp-aws-design.md` (Phase 4 Validation)

## Blocked by

- #489 (M2 Pipeline deploy)
- #483 (Redpanda module)

title: ADR-026 Phase 1: Structured proto types (FILTERED_MEAN, COMPOSITE, WINDOWED_COUNT) + M3 templates
description:
## Summary
Add three new structured `MetricType` enum values to the `MetricDefinition` proto — `FILTERED_MEAN`, `COMPOSITE`, `WINDOWED_COUNT` — and the corresponding Spark SQL templates in M3. Phase 1 of ADR-026; covers ~60% of CUSTOM metric use cases with full type-safe validation.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 1 sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `FILTERED_MEAN` added to `MetricType` enum with per-type config message (filter WHERE clause + value column)
- [ ] `COMPOSITE` added to `MetricType` enum with operands list + arithmetic operator
- [ ] `WINDOWED_COUNT` added to `MetricType` enum with event filter + time window spec
- [ ] M3 SQL templates: `filtered_mean.sql.tmpl`, `composite.sql.tmpl`, `windowed_count.sql.tmpl` in `services/metrics/internal/spark/templates/`
- [ ] Templates correctly emit exposure join, variant grouping, CUPED-compatible output (one row per user)
- [ ] `buf lint` and `buf breaking` pass
- [ ] Golden-file tests rendering each template against reference SQL

## Agent
Agent-3 (M3 Metrics) + Agent-4 (proto)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 1)

title: ADR-026 Phase 1: M5 validation for new structured metric types
description:
## Summary
M5 Management validation for the three new structured metric types (FILTERED_MEAN, COMPOSITE, WINDOWED_COUNT) added in Phase 1 of ADR-026. Reject malformed metric definitions at creation time.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 1 sections, plus `services/management/internal/validation/metric.go` for existing patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `FILTERED_MEAN`: filter WHERE clause parses as valid Spark SQL fragment, value column is non-empty
- [ ] `COMPOSITE`: operand metric_ids resolve to existing metrics, no cycles, operator is recognized
- [ ] `WINDOWED_COUNT`: window duration > 0, anchor event_type exists in event catalog
- [ ] Validation runs in `CreateMetric` and `UpdateMetric` RPCs
- [ ] Unit tests for each rejection case
- [ ] Contract test: Agent-3 receives validated definitions only

## Agent
Agent-5 (Management)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 1)

## Blocked By
ADR-026 Phase 1 proto types must land first

title: ADR-026 Phase 1: M6 UI for new structured metric types in metric creation form
description:
## Summary
M6 metric creation form gains UI affordances for the three new structured types (FILTERED_MEAN, COMPOSITE, WINDOWED_COUNT) from Phase 1 of ADR-026.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 1 sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `MetricType` dropdown adds the three new types
- [ ] FILTERED_MEAN: WHERE clause editor with syntax highlighting + value column picker
- [ ] COMPOSITE: operand metric picker (multi-select) + operator dropdown
- [ ] WINDOWED_COUNT: anchor event picker + time window selector
- [ ] Live validation against M5 (debounced)
- [ ] Form preview shows the rendered metric definition before save
- [ ] Existing metrics list shows the new types with appropriate icons/labels

## Agent
Agent-6 (UI)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 1)

## Blocked By
ADR-026 Phase 1 proto types + M5 validation must land first

title: ADR-026 Phase 2: MetricQL parser, AST, Spark SQL compiler with @metric_ref support
description:
## Summary
Build MetricQL — a purpose-built expression language for composed/windowed/funnel metrics. Phase 2 of ADR-026; covers the remaining ~35% of CUSTOM use cases that the structured types from Phase 1 cannot express.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 2 sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MetricQL grammar specified (EBNF or PEG)
- [ ] Parser produces typed AST with source-location info for diagnostics
- [ ] AST → Spark SQL compiler covers all grammar productions
- [ ] `@metric_ref` syntax compiles to subquery referencing another metric's definition
- [ ] Cycle detection on `@metric_ref` chains (reject A → B → A)
- [ ] Event type catalog lookup for event_type and column references
- [ ] Comprehensive golden-file tests (MetricQL input → expected Spark SQL)
- [ ] Shadow-run mode: emit both MetricQL-compiled and reference SQL, compare row outputs

## Agent
Agent-3 (M3 Metrics)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 2)

## Depends On
Event type catalog (built as part of this Issue or split into separate prerequisite)

title: ADR-026 Phase 2: M5 MetricQL validation + M6 expression editor
description:
## Summary
M5 validation accepts MetricQL expressions and runs them through the parser + semantic checker (event catalog lookup, type checking) before persistence. M6 surfaces a MetricQL editor with autocomplete and inline diagnostics.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 2 sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] M5: `expression` field on MetricDefinition accepts MetricQL strings
- [ ] M5: validation rejects parse errors and unresolved `@metric_ref` / event references with line:column diagnostics
- [ ] M5: validation includes cycle detection across the metric DAG
- [ ] M6: Monaco-based editor with MetricQL syntax highlighting
- [ ] M6: autocomplete on `@metric_` for existing metric names
- [ ] M6: inline diagnostics show parse errors and unresolved refs
- [ ] M6: dry-run preview shows compiled Spark SQL + sample output (server-rendered)

## Agent
Agent-5 (Management) + Agent-6 (UI)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 2)

## Blocked By
ADR-026 Phase 2 parser/compiler (Agent-3) must land first

title: ADR-026 Phase 3: Migrate existing CUSTOM metrics, deprecation warnings, UI removal
description:
## Summary
Phase 3 of ADR-026 — migrate existing CUSTOM metrics to structured (Tier 1) or MetricQL (Tier 2), surface deprecation warnings on new CUSTOM metric creation, and eventually remove the CUSTOM option from the M6 UI.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/026-custom-metrics-layer.md` Phase 3 sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration tool: scan existing CUSTOM metrics, classify each as Tier 1-eligible / Tier 2-eligible / Tier 3-fallback, produce a report
- [ ] For Tier 1/2-eligible CUSTOMs: shadow-run side-by-side and verify row-level output equivalence before suggesting migration
- [ ] M5: `CreateMetric` for CUSTOM type emits a deprecation warning (does NOT block)
- [ ] M5: `UpdateMetric` warns when changing to CUSTOM
- [ ] M6: CUSTOM option in metric creation form labeled "Deprecated — use Structured or MetricQL"
- [ ] Documentation: migration guide for each CUSTOM → Tier 1/2 pattern
- [ ] After 4-week sunset window: hide CUSTOM from M6 form (proto remains for read compatibility)

## Agent
Agent-3 (migration tool) + Agent-5 (deprecation logic) + Agent-6 (UI)

## ADR
ADR-026 (Phase 3)

## Blocked By
Phase 1 + Phase 2 must ship before migration becomes possible

title: ADR-027: TOST Equivalence Testing — Core Implementation
description:
## Summary
Implement Two One-Sided Tests (TOST) for proving statistical equivalence in infrastructure migration experiments.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/027-tost-equivalence-testing.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tost.rs`: `tost_equivalence_test()` with Welch's t internals
- [ ] `tost_cuped_equivalence_test()`: TOST composed with CUPED variance reduction
- [ ] `tost_sample_size()`: power analysis for equivalence designs
- [ ] Proto: `EquivalenceTestConfig` message with `delta`, `delta_relative`, `alpha`
- [ ] Golden-file: R TOSTER package to 6 decimal places
- [ ] Proptest: TOST p-value ≥ max(p_lower, p_upper); CI ⊂ [-δ,+δ] ↔ equivalent==true
- [ ] `cargo test -p experimentation-stats` passes

## Agent
Agent-4

## ADR
ADR-027

title: ADR-027: TOST — M5 Validation + M6 Equivalence Results View
description:
## Summary
M5 validation for equivalence experiments + M6 equivalence-specific results dashboard.

## Specification
Read `docs/adrs/027-tost-equivalence-testing.md` Sections 5–6

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] M5: validate delta > 0, delta_relative only for MEAN/RATIO metrics, power warning at creation
- [ ] M5: conclusion logic — 'equivalent → safe to migrate' instead of 'reject → ship treatment'
- [ ] M6: CI plot with [-δ, +δ] equivalence margin shaded region
- [ ] M6: Green/Yellow/Red badge (Equivalent / Inconclusive / Not Equivalent)
- [ ] M6: power indicator at current sample size

## Agent
Agent-5 (validation) + Agent-6 (UI)

## ADR
ADR-027

## Blocked By
ADR-027 core implementation (Agent-4) must land first
